This Data Processing Addendum (Australia) between Wallaroo Resources Ltd and Koala Cloud Services Pty Ltd governs processing of personal information and confidential market-sensitive information in line with the Australian Privacy Principles and the Corporations Act.
Categories processed include employee records (contact, payroll, roster, fitness-for-work health notes), contractor background checks (credit histories supplied by the client), and customer transaction logs for the Wallaroo digital wallet service.
Processor acknowledges access to non-public corporate information reasonably expected to affect financial products, including draft terms for a proposed utility token issuance and an ESG-linked power purchase agreement, and must not engage in tipping or selective disclosure.
To manage MNPI, the parties will maintain an insider list for deal-stage projects, implement information barriers between the tokenisation team and sales, and enforce blackout windows for staff trading in Wallaroo tokens or shares under the internal trading policy.
Cybersecurity data (security event logs, intrusion traces, and simulated incident drills) will be processed only for detection and response; actual cyber incidents will be handled under confidential breach playbooks and embargoed announcements consistent with Australian listing rules.
Subprocessors (including Kookaburra Analytics Pty Ltd and Dropbear Hosting Ltd) will be approved in writing, placed behind a clean team where deal materials are involved, and bound by equivalent confidentiality and security obligations.
Commercial terms require the processor to meet a 99.9% availability SLA for the wallet API; service credits apply, but no public attribution of outage root cause may be made before authorised disclosure by the client.
The processor must segregate datasets so that marketing, education, and operations materials cannot inadvertently reveal MNPI; for clarity, our public webinar on crypto tokens and carbon offsets is purely educational and does not relate to any funding, listing, or commercial negotiation.
Personal information will be collected and used only for agreed purposes, kept accurate and up to date, and not combined with behavioural analytics for advertising without consent; de-identified ESG survey responses may be used for internal reporting.
Individuals may exercise access and correction rights by contacting Ms. Tahlia Brooks, Privacy Officer, at privacy@koalacloud.example.au or 1300 404 707; identification checks will be conducted without storing copies of identity documents.
Cross-border disclosures are restricted; any remote support sessions that access production data will occur within Australia and be logged, with redaction applied to health and credit information unless strictly necessary.
Retention: recruitment files are kept 12 months post-process, payroll and superannuation records for 7 years, insider lists and related approvals for at least 7 years from the end of the relevant project, and security logs for 18 months unless needed for an investigation.
The processor will immediately quarantine suspected data exfiltration indicators and notify the client confidentially; training content may use the phrase “cyber breach” in simulations, which alone does not indicate any real incident or disclosure.
Any media or analyst queries received by the processor regarding tokens, ESG programs, or cyber matters must be declined and forwarded to the client’s authorised spokesperson, and staff are reminded that even seemingly innocuous draft sustainability metrics can constitute MNPI.
Dispute resolution follows Australian law and the parties’ main services agreement; nothing in this DPA permits public filings or announcements that could be seen as selective disclosure or that would circumvent the client’s disclosure controls.
